term_inventory_hebrew.meaning: To be anxious, fearful, worried, full of care; (Qal) to be anxious, to dread; used of anxious fear about impending calamity or uncertain outcome.
Dread, fear; feminine noun form of pa.chad; occurs only once, in Isa 24:17 in a triplet of terrors: the pit, the snare, and dread/fear as inescapable forces.

Dread, terror, fear; a strong visceral fear, often used of sudden overwhelming terror. Also used as a divine title: 'the Fear of Isaac' (Gen 31:42, 53) - a unique epithet for God as the one whom Isaac fears and reveres. Covers both the psychological state and its physical manifestation.
Fear, dread; noun form derived from the root gur (to dread). Used of fear that comes to pass - what one dreads happening. Rare term.
Fear, terror, dread; variant noun form of the same root as me.gu.rah and ma.gor. The two forms (H4034/H4035) are closely related; their distinction may be scribal or dialectal.

Fear, terror; the object of fear; in some contexts the awe-inspiring quality of God or a great person. Source file heading lists both mo.rah and mo.ra as alternative forms under H4172A (with H4172B noted for mo.rah). Primarily refers to reverential fear of God or the terror God inspires among nations.

Fear, reverence, awe, the fear of God; the primary noun for 'fear of the LORD' (yir.at YHWH) in the wisdom literature. Covers the full range from terrifying dread to reverential piety. Central to the OT wisdom tradition.
Fearful, afraid; adjective/participle form describing a person who is afraid.
Panic, terror, trembling fear; a hapax legomenon; describes sudden overwhelming terror - used in Jer 49:24 of Damascus seized with panic and anguish.

Razor; a cutting instrument. This term is a homograph with fear (mo.rah H4172A) but carries a different meaning - a razor used for shaving (Nazirite vow context). Included in the STEP fear word list likely due to shared spelling.
Shuddering, shaking, trembling with fear or horror; a strong somatic fear response; used of terrifying visions in the night and the fearsome appearance of the behemoth.

Terror, dread, horror; overwhelming fear sent by God or arising from a supernatural encounter. Used frequently for the terror that God sends upon enemies. Also used of the awe-inspiring darkness in the Abrahamic covenant (Gen 15:12).
Terror, dismay, shattered fear; from the root meaning to be shattered or broken; describes a state of dismay or terror that breaks the spirit.

Terror, dread, a source of terror; derived from the root gur (to dread). Used prominently in Jeremiah in the phrase 'terror on every side' (ma.gor mis.sa.bib) - a prophetic formula of overwhelming encircling threat, applied to Pashhur (Jer 20:3).
To be astonished, astounded, amazed; primarily a reaction of overwhelming wonder or shock, which may include a fear component; (Qal) to be astonished, stare in amazement.

To dread, be afraid, fear; (Qal) to dread, stand in awe of; STEP subdivided sense H1481C - the fear sense of the root gur. H1481A is the primary sense (to sojourn). The two senses share the same spelling.

To dread, tremble, fear, be in awe; (Qal) to be in dread, to be in awe; (Piel) to be in great dread; (Hiphil) to cause to dread. The verb form of pa.chad (H6343). Covers both instinctive terror and reverential awe.
To fear, be afraid; the fear sense (STEP H2119B) of the root zachal, which also means to crawl/creep. The Aramaic equivalent is de.chal (H1763). Source file notes ~7 occurrences; 9 verses present.

To fear; Biblical Aramaic verb; (P'al) to fear, to be afraid; passive participle: terrible, terrifying; (Pael) to cause to be afraid, make afraid. Used exclusively in the Aramaic sections of Daniel. Related to Hebrew za.chal.

verse_records.verse_text: When they came to the land of Zuph, Saul said to his servant who was with him, "Come, let us go back, lest my father cease to care about the donkeys and become anxious about us."

When you depart from me today, you will meet two men by Rachel's tomb in the territory of Benjamin at Zelzah, and they will say to you, 'The donkeys that you went to seek are found, and now your father has ceased to care about the donkeys and is anxious about you, saying, "What shall I do about my son?"'

He is like a tree planted by water, that sends out its roots by the stream, and does not fear when heat comes, for its leaves remain green, and is not anxious in the year of drought, for it does not cease to bear fruit.
And King Zedekiah said to Jeremiah, "I am afraid of the Judeans who have deserted to the Chaldeans, lest I be handed over to them and they deal cruelly with me."
then the sword that you fear shall overtake you there in the land of Egypt, and the famine of which you are afraid shall follow close after you to Egypt, and there you shall die.
Moreover, he said to me, "Son of man, behold, I will break the supply of bread in Jerusalem. They shall eat bread by weight and with anxiety, and they shall drink water by measure and in dismay.
"Son of man, eat your bread with quaking, and drink water with trembling and with anxiety,
Terror and the pit and the snare are upon you, O inhabitant of the earth!

If the God of my father, the God of Abraham and the Fear of Isaac, had not been on my side, surely now you would have sent me away empty-handed. God saw my affliction and the labor of my hands and rebuked you last night.
The God of Abraham and the God of Nahor, the God of their father, judge between us." So Jacob swore by the Fear of his father Isaac,
This day I will begin to put the dread and fear of you on the peoples who are under the whole heaven, who shall hear the report of you and shall tremble and be in anguish because of you.
No one shall be able to stand against you. The LORD your God will put the fear of you and the dread of you on all the land that you shall tread, as he promised you.
dread came upon me, and trembling, which made all my bones shake.
Dreadful sounds are in his ears; in prosperity the destroyer will come upon him.
Their houses are safe from fear, and no rod of God is upon them.
Therefore snares are all around you, and sudden terror overwhelms you,
There they are in great terror, for God is with the generation of the righteous.
Transgression speaks to the wicked deep in his heart; there is no fear of God before his eyes.
There they are, in great terror, where there is no terror! For God scatters the bones of him who encamps against you; you put them to shame, for God has rejected them.
I also will laugh at your calamity; I will mock when terror strikes you,
when terror strikes you like a storm and your calamity comes like a whirlwind, when distress and anguish come upon you.
Do not be afraid of sudden terror or of the ruin of the wicked, when it comes,
Enter into the rock and hide in the dust from before the terror of the LORD, and from the splendor of his majesty.
And people shall enter the caves of the rocks and the holes of the ground, from before the terror of the LORD, and from the splendor of his majesty, when he rises to terrify the earth.
to enter the caverns of the rocks and the clefts of the cliffs, from before the terror of the LORD, and from the splendor of his majesty, when he rises to terrify the earth.
Terror and the pit and the snare are upon you, O inhabitant of the earth!

He who flees at the sound of the terror shall fall into the pit, and he who climbs out of the pit shall be caught in the snare. For the windows of heaven are opened, and the foundations of the earth tremble.

Your evil will chastise you, and your apostasy will reprove you. Know and see that it is evil and bitter for you to forsake the LORD your God; the fear of me is not in you, declares the Lord GOD of hosts.
"Thus says the LORD: We have heard a cry of panic, of terror, and no peace.
Terror, pit, and snare are before you, O inhabitant of Moab! declares the LORD.

He who flees from the terror shall fall into the pit, and he who climbs out of the pit shall be caught in the snare. For I will bring these things upon Moab, the year of their punishment, declares the LORD.
panic and pitfall have come upon us, devastation and destruction;

Then all the princes of the sea will step down from their thrones and remove their robes and strip off their embroidered garments. They will clothe themselves with trembling; they will sit on the ground and tremble every moment and be appalled at you.
Now the coastlands tremble on the day of your fall, and the coastlands that are on the sea are dismayed at your passing.'
"On that day messengers shall go out from me in ships to terrify the unsuspecting people of Cush, and anguish shall come upon them on the day of Egypt's doom; for, behold, it comes!

I will make many peoples appalled at you, and the hair of their kings shall bristle with horror because of you, when I brandish my sword before them. They shall tremble every moment, every one for his own life, on the day of your downfall.
Afterward the children of Israel shall return and seek the LORD their God, and David their king, and they shall come in fear to the LORD and to his goodness in the latter days.

they shall lick the dust like a serpent, like the crawling things of the earth; they shall come trembling out of their strongholds; they shall turn in dread to the LORD our God, and they shall be in fear of you.
What the wicked dreads will come upon him, but the desire of the righteous will be granted.

I also will choose harsh treatment for them and bring their fears upon them, because when I called, no one answered, when I spoke, they did not listen; but they did the evil in my eyes and chose that in which I did not delight.
I sought the LORD, and he answered me and delivered me from all my fears.
Fear and trembling come upon me, and horror overwhelms me.

The fear of you and the dread of you shall be upon every beast of the earth and upon every bird of the heavens, upon everything that creeps on the ground and all the fish of the sea. Into your hand they are delivered.

Or has any god ever attempted to go and take a nation for himself from the midst of another nation, by trials, by signs, by wonders, and by war, by a mighty hand and an outstretched arm, and by great deeds of terror, all of which the LORD your God did for you in Egypt before your eyes?
and the LORD brought us out of Egypt with a mighty hand and an outstretched arm, with great deeds of terror, with signs and wonders.
and for all the mighty power and all the great deeds of terror that Moses did in the sight of all Israel.
Do you make him leap like the locust? His majestic snorting is terrifying.
Put them in fear, O LORD! Let the nations know that they are but men! Selah
Make your vows to the LORD your God and perform them; let all around him bring gifts to him who is to be feared,
But the LORD of hosts, him you shall honor as holy. Let him be your fear, and let him be your dread.
and brought your people Israel out of the land of Egypt with signs and wonders, with a strong hand and outstretched arm, and with great terror.

"A son honors his father, and a servant his master. If then I am a father, where is my honor? And if I am a master, where is my fear? says the LORD of hosts to you, O priests, who despise my name. But you say, 'How have we despised your name?'
My covenant with him was one of life and peace, and I gave them to him. It was a covenant of fear, and he feared me. He stood in awe of my name.
Abraham said, "I did it because I thought, There is no fear of God at all in this place, and they will kill me because of my wife.
Moses said to the people, "Do not fear, for God has come to test you, that the fear of him may be before you, that you may not sin."
Every one of you shall revere his mother and his father, and you shall keep my Sabbaths: I am the LORD your God.
This day I will begin to put the dread and fear of you on the peoples who are under the whole heaven, who shall hear the report of you and shall tremble and be in anguish because of you.

how on the day that you stood before the LORD your God at Horeb, the LORD said to me, 'Gather the people to me, that I may let them hear my words, so that they may learn to fear me all the days that they live on the earth, and that they may teach their children so.'

that you may fear the LORD your God, you and your son and your son's son, by keeping all his statutes and his commandments, which I command you, all the days of your life, and that your days may be long.
It is the LORD your God you shall fear. Him you shall serve and by his name you shall swear.
And the LORD commanded us to do all these statutes, to fear the LORD our God, for our good always, that he might preserve us alive, as we are this day.
So you shall keep the commandments of the LORD your God by walking in his ways and by fearing him.

"And now, Israel, what does the LORD your God require of you, but to fear the LORD your God, to walk in all his ways, to love him, to serve the LORD your God with all your heart and with all your soul,
so that all the peoples of the earth may know that the hand of the LORD is mighty, that you may fear the LORD your God forever.
And when the people saw the message, the fear of the LORD fell upon the people, and they came out as one man.
And he charged them: "Thus you shall do in the fear of the LORD, in faithfulness, and with your whole heart:
Is not your fear of God your confidence, and the integrity of your ways your hope?
He who withholds kindness from a friend forsakes the fear of the Almighty.
But you are doing away with the fear of God and hindering meditation before God.
Is it for your fear of him that he reproves you and enters into judgment with you?
And he said to man, 'Behold, the fear of the Lord, that is wisdom, and to turn away from evil is understanding.'
Serve the LORD with fear, and rejoice with trembling.
But I, through the abundance of your steadfast love, will enter your house. I will bow down toward your holy temple in the fear of you.
the fear of the LORD is clean, enduring forever; the rules of the LORD are true, and righteous altogether.
You who fear the LORD, praise him! All you offspring of Jacob, glorify him, and stand in awe of him, all you offspring of Israel!
The friendship of the LORD is for those who fear him, and he makes known to them his covenant.
Oh, how abundant is your goodness, which you have stored up for those who fear you and worked for those who take refuge in you, in the sight of the children of mankind!
Let all the earth fear the LORD; let all the inhabitants of the world stand in awe of him!
Behold, the eye of the LORD is on those who fear him, on those who hope in his steadfast love,
The angel of the LORD encamps around those who fear him, and delivers them.
Oh, fear the LORD, you his saints, for those who fear him have no lack!
Come, O children, listen to me; I will teach you the fear of the LORD.
The fear of the LORD is the beginning of knowledge; fools despise wisdom and instruction.
The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom, and knowledge of the Holy One is insight.
In the fear of the LORD one has strong confidence, and his children will have a refuge.
The fear of the LORD is a fountain of life, that one may turn away from the snares of death.
The fear of the LORD is instruction in wisdom, and humility comes before honor.
The fear of the LORD leads to life, and whoever has it rests satisfied; he will not be visited by harm.
The reward for humility and fear of the LORD is riches and honor and life.
And the Spirit of the LORD shall rest upon him, the Spirit of wisdom and understanding, the Spirit of counsel and might, the Spirit of knowledge and the fear of the LORD.
And his delight shall be in the fear of the LORD. He shall not judge by what his eyes see, or decide disputes by what his ears hear,
and he will be the stability of your times, abundance of salvation, wisdom, and knowledge; the fear of the LORD is Zion's treasure.
O LORD, why do you make us wander from your ways and harden our heart, so that we fear you not? Return for the sake of your servants, the tribes of your heritage.

Your evil will chastise you, and your apostasy will reprove you. Know and see that it is evil and bitter for you to forsake the LORD your God; the fear of me is not in you, declares the Lord GOD of hosts.
I will make with them an everlasting covenant, that I will not turn away from doing good to them. And I will put the fear of me in their hearts, that they may not turn from me.
So the church throughout all Judea and Galilee and Samaria had peace and was being built up. And walking in the fear of the Lord and in the comfort of the Holy Spirit, it multiplied.
And he will bring upon you again all the diseases of Egypt, of which you were afraid, and they shall cling to you.
"I, I am he who comforts you; who are you that you are afraid of man who dies, of the son of man who is made like grass,
Damascus has become feeble, she turned to flee, and panic seized her; anguish and sorrows have taken hold of her, as of a woman in labor.

for behold, you shall conceive and bear a son. No razor shall come upon his head, for the child shall be a Nazirite to God from the womb, and he shall begin to save Israel from the hand of the Philistines.

She made a vow and said, "O LORD of hosts, if you will indeed look on the affliction of your servant and remember me and not forget your servant, but will give to your servant a son, then I will give him to the LORD all the days of his life, and no razor shall touch his head."
All the men of Israel, when they saw the man, fled from him and were much afraid.
who shakes the earth out of its place, and its pillars tremble;
When I remember, I am dismayed, and shuddering seizes my flesh.
My heart staggers; horror has appalled me; the twilight I longed for has been turned for me into trembling.
They put on sackcloth, and horror covers them. Shame is on all faces, and baldness on all their heads.
As the sun was going down, a deep sleep fell on Abram. And behold, dreadful and great darkness fell upon him.
Terror and dread fall upon them; because of the greatness of your arm, they are still as a stone, till your people, O LORD, pass by, till the people pass by whom you have purchased.
I will send my terror before you and will throw into confusion all the people against whom you shall come, and I will make all your enemies turn their backs to you.
Outdoors the sword bereaves, and indoors terror, for young man and woman alike, the nursing child with the man of gray hairs.
Let him take his rod away from me, and let not dread of him terrify me.
withdraw your hand far from me, and let not dread of you terrify me.
It is drawn forth and comes out of his body; the glittering point comes out of his gallbladder; terrors come upon him.
Behold, no fear of me need terrify you; my pressure will not be heavy upon you.
Do you make him leap like the locust? His majestic snorting is terrifying.
Who can open the doors of his face? Around his teeth is terror.
My heart is in anguish within me; the terrors of death have fallen upon me.
How they are destroyed in a moment, swept away utterly by terrors!
Afflicted and close to death from my youth up, I suffer your terrors; I am helpless.
The terror of a king is like the growling of a lion; whoever provokes him to anger forfeits his life.
Your heart will muse on the terror: "Where is he who counted, where is he who weighed the tribute? Where is he who counted the towers?"
whose graves are set in the uttermost parts of the pit, with all her multitude around her grave; all of them slain, fallen by the sword, who spread terror in the land of the living.

And they do not lie with the mighty men of old who went down to Sheol with their weapons of war, whose swords were laid under their heads, and whose iniquities are upon their bones; for the terror of the mighty men was in the land of the living.
On earth there is not his like, a creature without fear.
In righteousness you shall be established; you shall be far from oppression, for you shall not fear; and from terror, for it shall not come near you.
For I hear the whispering of many - terror on every side! - as they scheme together against me, as they plot to take my life.
Do not go out into the field, nor walk on the road, for the enemy has a sword; terror is on every side.
On the next day, when Pashhur released Jeremiah from the stocks, Jeremiah said to him, "The LORD does not call your name Pashhur, but Terror on Every Side.

For I hear many whispering. Terror is on every side! "Denounce him! Let us denounce him!" say all my close friends, watching for my fall. "Perhaps he will be deceived; then we can overcome him and take our revenge on him."
Why have I seen it? They are dismayed and have turned backward. Their warriors are beaten down and have fled in haste; they look not back - terror on every side! declares the LORD.
Their tents and their flocks shall be taken, their curtains and all their goods; their camels shall be led away from them, and men shall cry to them: 'Terror on every side!'
You summoned as if to a festival day my terrors on every side, and on the day of the anger of the LORD no one escaped or survived; those whom I held and raised my enemy destroyed.
whose graves are set in the uttermost parts of the pit, with all her multitude around her grave; all of them slain, fallen by the sword, who spread terror in the land of the living.
And they sat before him, the firstborn according to his birthright and the youngest according to his youth. And the men looked at one another in amazement.
As soon as they saw it, they were astounded; they were in panic; they took to flight.
Princes persecute me without cause, but my heart stands in awe of your words.
They will be dismayed: pangs and agony will seize them; they will be in anguish like a woman in labor. They will look aghast at one another; their faces will be aflame.
Astonish yourselves and be astonished; blind yourselves and be blind! Be drunk, but not with wine; stagger, but not with strong drink!
"In that day, declares the LORD, courage shall fail both king and officials. The priests shall be appalled and the prophets astounded."
Warn the nations that he is coming; announce to Jerusalem, "Besiegers come from a distant land; they shout against the cities of Judah.
"Look among the nations, and see; wonder and be astounded. For I am doing a work in your days that you would not believe if told.
And Moab was in great dread of the people, because they were many. Moab was overcome with fear of the people of Israel.

You shall not be partial in judgment. You shall hear the small and the great alike. You shall not be intimidated by anyone, for the judgment is God's. And the case that is too hard for you, you shall bring to me, and I will hear it.
had I not feared provocation by the enemy, lest their adversaries should misunderstand, lest they should say, 'Our hand is triumphant, it was not the LORD who did all this.'
And when Saul saw that he had great success, he stood in fearful awe of him.
be afraid of the sword, for wrath brings the punishment of the sword, that you may know there is a judgment.
When he raises himself up, the mighty are afraid; at the crashing they are beside themselves.

and have forgotten the LORD, your Maker, who stretched out the heavens and laid the foundations of the earth, and you fear continually all the day because of the wrath of the oppressor, when he sets himself to destroy? And where is the wrath of the oppressor?
Your life shall hang in doubt before you. Night and day you shall be in dread and have no assurance of your life.

In the morning you shall say, 'If only it were evening!' and at evening you shall say, 'If only it were morning!' because of the dread that your heart shall feel, and the sights that your eyes shall see.
For the thing that I fear comes upon me, and what I dread befalls me.
dread came upon me, and trembling, which made all my bones shake.
Therefore I am terrified at his presence; when I consider, I am in dread of him.
There they are in great terror, for God is with the generation of the righteous.
The LORD is my light and my salvation; whom shall I fear? The LORD is the stronghold of my life; of whom shall I be afraid?
There they are, in great terror, where there is no terror! For God scatters the bones of him who encamps against you; you put them to shame, for God has rejected them.
He led them in safety, so that they were not afraid, but the sea overwhelmed their enemies.
Princes persecute me without cause, but my heart stands in awe of your words.
If you lie down, you will not be afraid; when you lie down, your sleep will be sweet.
Blessed is the one who fears the LORD always, but whoever hardens his heart will fall into calamity.
"Behold, God is my salvation; I will trust, and will not be afraid; for the LORD GOD is my strength and my song, and he has become my salvation."
In that day the Egyptians will be like women, and tremble with fear before the hand that the LORD of hosts shakes over them.
And the land of Judah will become a terror to the Egyptians. Everyone to whom it is mentioned will fear because of the purpose that the LORD of hosts has purposed against them.
The sinners in Zion are afraid; trembling has seized the godless: "Who among us can dwell with the consuming fire? Who among us can dwell with everlasting burnings?"
Fear not, nor be afraid; have I not told you from of old and declared it? And you are my witnesses! Is there a God besides me? There is no Rock; I know not any."
Behold, all his companions shall be put to shame, and the craftsmen are only human. Let them all assemble, let them stand forth. They shall be terrified; they shall be put to shame together.

and have forgotten the LORD, your Maker, who stretched out the heavens and laid the foundations of the earth, and you fear continually all the day because of the wrath of the oppressor, when he sets himself to destroy? And where is the wrath of the oppressor?
Then you shall see and be radiant; your heart shall thrill and exult, because the abundance of the sea shall be turned to you, the wealth of the nations shall come to you.

And this city shall be to me a name of joy, a praise and a glory before all the nations of the earth who shall hear of all the good that I do for them. They shall fear and tremble because of all the good and all the prosperity I provide for it.
When they heard all the words, they turned one to another in fear. And they said to Baruch, "We must report all these words to the king."
Yet neither the king nor any of his servants who heard all these words was afraid, nor did they tear their garments.
Afterward the children of Israel shall return and seek the LORD their God, and David their king, and they shall come in fear to the LORD and to his goodness in the latter days.

they shall lick the dust like a serpent, like the crawling things of the earth; they shall come trembling out of their strongholds; they shall turn in dread to the LORD our God, and they shall be in fear of you.
And Elihu the son of Barachel the Buzite answered and said: "I am young in years, and you are aged; therefore I was timid and afraid to declare my opinion to you.
"You saw, O king, and behold, a great image. This image, mighty and of exceeding brightness, stood before you, and its appearance was frightening.
I saw a dream that made me afraid. As I lay in bed the fancies and the visions of my head alarmed me.

And because of the greatness that he gave him, all peoples, nations, and languages trembled and feared before him. Whom he would, he killed, and whom he would, he kept alive; whom he would, he raised up, and whom he would, he humbled.

I make a decree, that in all my royal dominion people are to tremble and fear before the God of Daniel, for he is the living God, enduring forever; his kingdom shall never be destroyed, and his dominion shall be to the end.

After this I saw in the night visions, and behold, a fourth beast, terrifying and dreadful and exceedingly strong. It had great iron teeth; it devoured and broke in pieces and stamped what was left with its feet. It was different from all the beasts that were before it, and it had ten horns.

they shall lick the dust like a serpent, like the crawling things of the earth; they shall come trembling out of their strongholds; they shall turn in dread to the LORD our God, and they shall be in fear of you.
And Elihu the son of Barachel the Buzite answered and said: "I am young in years, and you are aged; therefore I was timid and afraid to declare my opinion to you.
"You saw, O king, and behold, a great image. This image, mighty and of exceeding brightness, stood before you, and its appearance was frightening.
I saw a dream that made me afraid. As I lay in bed the fancies and the visions of my head alarmed me.

And because of the greatness that he gave him, all peoples, nations, and languages trembled and feared before him. Whom he would, he killed, and whom he would, he kept alive; whom he would, he raised up, and whom he would, he humbled.

I make a decree, that in all my royal dominion people are to tremble and fear before the God of Daniel, for he is the living God, enduring forever; his kingdom shall never be destroyed, and his dominion shall be to the end.

After this I saw in the night visions, and behold, a fourth beast, terrifying and dreadful and exceedingly strong. It had great iron teeth; it devoured and broke in pieces and stamped what was left with its feet. It was different from all the beasts that were before it, and it had ten horns.

"Then I desired to know the truth about the fourth beast, which was different from all the rest, exceedingly terrifying, with its teeth of iron and claws of bronze, and which devoured and broke in pieces and stamped what was left with its feet,

data_quality_flags.description: to dread (gur H1481C): the verse block in the source file contained approximately 50+ verses for H1481A (to sojourn) mixed with H1481C (to dread) verses. Only fear-relevant verses extracted.
to dread (gur H1481C): ~10 occurrences stated; 7 fear-relevant verses extracted after removal of H1481A sojourn verses.
dread (pa.chad H6343): ~49 occurrences stated; approximately 30 verses extracted. Less than 20% threshold.
razor (mo.rah H4177): primary meaning is razor, not fear. Included in STEP fear word list due to homograph with mo.rah (H4172A). Fear domain relevance uncertain. Phase 2 to assess.
to astound (ta.mah H8539): ~9 occurrences stated; 8 verses extracted. Primary meaning is astonishment, not fear. Phase 2 to assess domain relevance.

fear (mo.rah/mo.ra): MTI provisional number H4172B corrected. Source file uses H4172A as the primary entry with H4172B noted as an alternate form. mo.ra recorded as also_spelled under H4172A. MTI entry H4172B to be removed or merged.
fear (me.gu.rah): MTI provisional number H4033A corrected to source-file H4035.
fear (me.go.rah): MTI provisional number H4033B corrected to source-file H4034.
dread noun (pa.chad): MTI provisional number H6343B corrected to source-file H6343.
dread noun (pach.dah): MTI provisional number H6343A corrected to source-file H6345.
panic (re.tet): MTI provisional number H7578 corrected to source-file H7374.
terror (ma.gor): MTI provisional number H4036 corrected to source-file H4032.
to fear (de.chal H1763): Biblical Aramaic term. Verse overlap with za.chal expected as STEP may tag Daniel Aramaic verses under both H2119 and H1763.
